title: Rich Crisp Filipino Brie Frittata
description: A lacto-vegetarian friendly Filipino dish featuring brie, perfect for a brunch mood, with umami and crisp textures. This frittata combines the richness of brie with the brightness of Filipino flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Add the softened brie cheese to the egg mixture and stir until the cheese is fully incorporated.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced longanisa to the skillet and cook until browned, about 3 minutes.
Add the chopped green onions and minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Pour the egg mixture over the longanisa mixture in the skillet.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the frittata is golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Slice the frittata into wedges and serve hot.
